quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, visible from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in 1592. The waterway connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and is home to orcas, gray whales, and harbor seals that migrate through its waters each season.

short_read.title: Why Do Hummingbirds Hum?
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world create their distinctive sound not with their voices, but with their incredibly fast wingbeats. Discover the science behind one of nature's most delightful sounds.

medium_read.title: The Thermal Baths of Budapest: A Living Tradition
medium_read.teaser: For over 2,000 years, Hungarians have gathered in thermal baths to relax, socialize, and heal. This beautiful photo essay explores the timeless tradition of Budapest's famous bathhouses and the culture that surrounds them.

long_read.title: The Secret Life of Trees
long_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that trees communicate with each other through underground fungal networks, sharing nutrients and even warning each other of danger. This fascinating article reveals the hidden social world of forests.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot black-tailed deer grazing in the meadows at Hurricane Ridge. These gentle creatures are especially active in the early morning and evening, often seen with their spotted fawns during this season. The drive up offers spectacular views of the Olympic Mountains and wildflowers in bloom.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! You know your hummingbird facts! Their tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power those rapid wings.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! Those tiny hearts beat about 1,200 times per minute — that's about 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those amazing little birds have hearts that beat about 1,200 times per minute in flight — faster than almost any other animal!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible heart rate, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in nectar each day. They visit hundreds of flowers daily and can remember every flower they've visited!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly — such a lovely, flowing word for such a delicate creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound as graceful as the creature itself? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite unique in Europe. Many Hungarian words have a musical, flowing quality that reflects the beauty of what they describe.

puzzle_3.question: What do gardeners call the practice of planting certain crops together for mutual benefit?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Companion planting is a time-honored gardening tradition. Tomatoes love basil, carrots enjoy onions, and beans help corn grow stronger!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The answer is companion planting! This ancient technique pairs plants that help each other — like the 'Three Sisters' of corn, beans, and squash that Native Americans planted together for centuries.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Companion planting can repel pests naturally, improve soil nutrition, and increase yields. Marigolds planted near vegetables deter many harmful insects, while nasturtiums attract aphids away from other plants.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Companion planting guide — Old Farmer's Almanac

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
